findings.finding: Deposit endpoint correctly validates: session auth (requireSessionApi), campaign ownership (creatorId check), ID format (SAFE_ID_REGEX), amount validation (number, finite, > 0). No path traversal vectors.
findings.reason: All security boundaries are enforced before any file modification.

findings.finding: Approve endpoint correctly enforces: session auth, submission exists in creator's data, campaign ownership via submission.campaignId -> campaign.creatorId === userId, submission must be pending. No IDOR vulnerability.
findings.reason: Ownership chain is correctly verified: user -> campaign -> submission. Cannot approve submissions from other users' campaigns.

findings.finding: Deposit amount validation accepts any positive number including very large values (e.g., Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER). No upper bound check.
findings.reason: Points are an internal system — there is no real currency at stake in MVP. Creator can only deposit to their own escrow. Adding an upper bound would be arbitrary and could break future use cases. Number.isFinite already prevents Infinity.

findings.finding: escrowPath uses template literal with campaignId. campaignId is validated against SAFE_ID_REGEX before any endpoint uses it, preventing path traversal.
findings.reason: isValidId check happens at the beginning of every endpoint handler before escrowPath is called.

summary: No security issues found. All endpoints enforce authentication, ownership, and input validation. Path traversal prevented by SAFE_ID_REGEX. No IDOR vulnerabilities in the approve flow. Race condition handling follows Decision 7 (accepted limitation).
